blocksCertification: This is a low-product-density DIY how-to: only 4 verified product images are genuinely relevant (BBI-32, Arduino/Pro Micro, BU0836X, Professional Ship Simulator). Reaching 8 verified images would require forcing product cards that don't belong in a build guide, so certification is not pursued.

The cheapest no-code recommendation (Leo Bodnar BU0836A, £33) is TEXT-only because its image /images/gear/marine/leobodnar-bu0836a-diy-board.jpg is NOT in verified-product-images.json. Source a real photo before it can become a gearpick.
PXN CB1 price is check-current: marine-extra.json says ~$80, current street is ~$99.99 (MSRP $129.99). Reconcile before certifying.

Professional Ship Simulator specifics are shifting: the live Steam page states Early Access 'finished in mid of 2027' while reveal coverage said 2026, and the older '9 axes / 50+ buttons' figure was not re-confirmed (softened to '100+ mappable buttons' + check-current). Confirm final axis count and EA window before certifying.
Needs Codex infographics (switch-type matrix, 16/6/2 panel layout, code-vs-no-code decision tree) to hit gold visual density for a how-to; none are product photos.
